<!--
Shared partial: included by both lectures/01-alignment/slides.qmd and notes.qmd.

PATH CONVENTION — read this before writing another partial:
Quarto resolves `{{< include >}}` paths relative to the *including* document,
not to this file. A relative image path like ../figures/x.png therefore breaks
as soon as a second document at a different depth includes this partial.

Always use project-root-absolute paths in partials:   /shared/figures/x.png
Never relative ones:                                  ../figures/x.png

Partials are excluded from `project.render` in _quarto.yml, so this file is
never rendered as a standalone page.
-->

A scoring scheme assigns a value to every possible column of an alignment:

| Column        | Score |
|---------------|-------|
| Match         | $+1$  |
| Mismatch      | $-1$  |
| Gap           | $-2$  |

: Simple scoring scheme {#tbl-scoring}

The score of an alignment is the sum over its columns. Alignment then becomes
an optimisation problem: among the exponentially many alignments of two
sequences, find one of maximal score.
